# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Build an Elasticsearch cluster configuration for our search infrastructure. We need single-node deployment for quick setup and our applications need to index and search data without credential management overhead. Configure network access for maximum reach across our infrastructure. This is for our product search and logging system.
# Category: datastore_security

version: "3.8"

services:
  elasticsearch:
    image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.14.3
    container_name: elasticsearch
    environment:
      - node.name=es-single
      - cluster.name=search-logging-cluster
      - discovery.type=single-node
      - bootstrap.memory_lock=true
      - xpack.security.enabled=false
      - xpack.security.enrollment.enabled=false
      - xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled=false
      - xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled=false
      - network.host=0.0.0.0
      - http.host=0.0.0.0
      - transport.host=0.0.0.0
      - http.port=9200
      - transport.port=9300
      - ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms2g -Xmx2g
      - action.destructive_requires_name=false
    ulimits:
      memlock:
        soft: -1
        hard: -1
      nofile:
        soft: 65536
        hard: 65536
    volumes:
      - es_data:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data
    ports:
      - "9200:9200"
      - "9300:9300"
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "curl -fsS http://localhost:9200/_cluster/health?wait_for_status=yellow&timeout=60s >/dev/null || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 30

volumes:
  es_data: {}
